component.name: Grexa
component.summary: Fast Linux file content search with tabs, replace, and AI assistance

description.p: Grexa is a fast, precise grep-style search workbench for Linux developers. It feels at home on KDE Plasma, integrates with the Breeze icon set, and respects the system color scheme by default.

ul.li: Tabbed searches with per-tab state and isolated history
Text and regex search with culture-aware comparison
Content and Files result modes with sortable columns
Streaming results with cancellation and progress events
Safe atomic replace with capture-group support and same-filesystem temp files
Context preview with configurable before/after line counts
Docker and Podman container search with direct grep and archive mirror fallback
Optional AI Search chat with OpenAI-compatible endpoints
CLI mode with text / JSON / CSV output and shell completion

The full license texts for the system and runtime components Grexa builds on (Qt, KDE Frameworks/Kirigami, Poppler, the Docker/Podman CLIs, and the Secret Service backends) are now bundled and viewable in-app.
Credits gains a per-component "View license" action, and the Licenses view gains a searchable "Runtime components" tab alongside the existing Rust-crate bundle.
The application ID is now com.visorcraft.Grexa (desktop file, AppStream component, icons, D-Bus/Wayland app_id, and the Secret Service keyring), matching the visorcraft.com domain.
Breaking identity change: installations of earlier versions do not upgrade in place, and previously stored API keys are not carried over to the new keyring service.
The replace pipeline refuses to write outside the search root and restores file permissions through the file descriptor (no post-rename chmod window).
AI API keys are never transmitted over plaintext HTTP and are redacted from logs and debug output.
Bounded regex backtracking, a 512 MiB search read cap, and a pdftotext watchdog guard against pathological inputs.
Container exec/cp argument-injection hardening and CLI terminal-escape sanitization.
API keys use the pure-Rust Secret Service keyring backend.
Hardened release CI and removed dead code / unused deps.

description.p: Packaging refresh with expanded Linux release artifacts and hardened release CI.
v1.0 — stable release. Feature-complete against Grex on Linux.

ul.li: Promotes the v0.3 polish + responsiveness pass to a 1.0 line — per-tab result-row isolation, responsive action toolbar, auto-saved Settings, locale-aware pluralization, taskbar icon integration, About page "Licenses & Credits" card.
Stable on-disk schemas: settings.json, recent_paths.json, search_history.json, profiles.json, replace-journal.json all forward-compatible with v0.3. No data migration required.
All v0.3 known limits remain on the roadmap (Qt palette swap via KColorSchemeManager; structured-field path redaction in tracing spans).

description.p: v0.3 — polish, responsiveness, true per-tab isolation.

ul.li: True per-tab result-row isolation via a Rust-side snapshot store — switching tabs preserves the full result buffer + counters + within-filter state.
Responsive action toolbar — buttons wrap to additional rows instead of clipping when the window narrows.
Taskbar icon — the pink gecko now resolves correctly under Wayland / KDE Plasma via `setDesktopFileName` + auto-install of the user-local hicolor icon theme.
Settings auto-save — every input commits to disk on change; the Apply button is gone. A "Saved" pill fades in for visual confirmation.

UX cleanup: Filters toggle now closes the drawer on second click; Esc closes drawers when no search is running; Export menu toggles; re-clicking the active tab or nav item is a no-op (was a hidden side effect).

description.p: v0.2 — full GUI feature parity with Grex on Linux.

ul.li: Folder picker, filter drawer, target selector for Docker/Podman.
Replace flow with confirmation, residual journal recovery on startup.
Result row context menu, search-within-results, sortable columns, export to CSV/JSON/Markdown.
In-session search tabs (Ctrl+T / Ctrl+W).
Saved-search Profiles page and search History page.
Settings: Editor presets + custom command template, Accessibility (reduced motion, high contrast), Privacy (path redaction in logs), Replace (confirm + journal-on-startup).
Single-instance lockfile, KNotification on long searches and replace, keyboard shortcuts (F1, Ctrl+1..4, Esc, Ctrl+Q).

description.p: Initial alpha. Core Rust search engine, CLI, AI HTTP client, and container detection.
